statement: If self-adjoint bounded operators S_N converge to S in norm and a contour Gamma separates an isolated spectral cluster of S, then the Riesz projections converge in norm for all large N.
hypotheses: S_N and S are bounded self-adjoint operators.
Gamma lies in the resolvent set of S and encloses the selected cluster with positive distance to the rest of the spectrum.

resolventIdentityStatus.label: resolvent identity on a fixed contour
resolventIdentityStatus.reason: For z on Gamma, (z-S_N)^{-1}-(z-S)^{-1}=(z-S_N)^{-1}(S_N-S)(z-S)^{-1} once ||S_N-S|| is below the contour distance.

rieszProjectionContinuityStatus.label: Riesz projection norm continuity
rieszProjectionContinuityStatus.reason: Integrating the resolvent identity over Gamma gives ||P_N-P|| -> 0.

rankPersistenceStatus.label: spectral rank persistence
rankPersistenceStatus.reason: Norm-small perturbations preserve the rank enclosed by the contour.

proof: Choose a contour Gamma at positive distance from sigma(S).
For N large, ||S_N-S|| is smaller than half that distance, so Gamma also lies in the resolvent set of S_N.
Use the resolvent identity and uniform resolvent bounds on Gamma.
Integrate around Gamma to obtain norm convergence of the Riesz projections and rank persistence.
